title: Sensor drift and flatline in humidity monitoring
summary: A key humidity sensor appears to be drifting or flatlining, reducing trust in climate decisions for Sector B during active production.
suspected_issue: The main humidity sensing path for Sector B may be producing misleading data due to drift or flatline behavior.
symptoms: The reported humidity value remains almost unchanged despite environmental conditions that should create variation.
Field observations suggest that actual moisture conditions do not match the reported digital reading.
Climate decisions for Sector B are becoming less reliable because a key signal may be misleading.
The crop is in production phase and delayed correction may increase disease-risk exposure.

observed_signals.interpretation: The humidity reading appears suspiciously stable and may not reflect real environmental variation.
Temperature is plausible, but it does not remove the concern that the humidity sensor may be misleading.
Communication appears normal, which suggests the issue is more likely sensor quality than telemetry outage.

operational_context.note: Sector B climate decisions currently depend on this humidity sensing path.
operational_context.impact: The service should avoid overconfidence because a misleading sensor can drive wrong operational actions.

operational_context.note: No explicit backup sensing path is confirmed for humidity in this sector.
operational_context.impact: The service should emphasize verification and cautious interpretation rather than assuming a clean fallback.

agronomic_context.note: The crop is in production phase and sustained hidden humidity problems can increase disease pressure.
agronomic_context.impact: A misleading sensor may delay response and expose the crop to avoidable phytosanitary risk.

business_context.note: Poor sensor trust affects the reliability of greenhouse decisions even if actuator hardware is still functional.
business_context.impact: The business impact comes from acting on misleading data rather than from a confirmed actuator failure.

notes: This case is designed to test whether Agro-DO responds prudently when the core issue is misleading sensor behavior rather than physical failure, backup availability, or full communication loss.
